page: Microsoft Azure F64als_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Falsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores and 64 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with an x86_64 architecture. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz and includes 48 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16, alongside cryptography standards like AES and SHA_NI.

The server is configured with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. This allocation represents a specific tradeoff, prioritizing raw compute density over memory capacity. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, though it is premium storage capable, requiring external network-attached storage for persistent data. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included.

In benchmark evaluations, the F64als_v7 demonstrates top-tier performance, placing in the top 10% for both single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU tests. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with cached small-block reads reaching 11979.01 GB/sec and uncached large-block reads at 410.03 GB/sec. The processor's vector extensions contribute to top-tier performance in LLM inference, achieving 316.09 tokens/sec for prompt processing on a medium 7B model and 5.06 tokens/sec for text generation on a large 70B model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the F64als_v7 offers high compute density per resource unit, making it a viable option for CPU-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. It is suited for compute-intensive tasks, batch processing, static web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure F64als_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Falsv7 family, powered by a dedicated 64-core AMD EPYC 9V45 processor operating at 4.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, it features 128.0 GB of RAM, establishing a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage but supports premium storage options. In benchmark testing, the F64als_v7 delivers top-tier performance, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng CPU tests, static web serving at 103.82 GB/sec, and Redis SET operations at 171.03 million operations per second. It also demonstrates strong capabilities in CPU-based LLM inference. This server is designed for compute-heavy workloads, batch processing, and high-throughput web serving where high CPU frequency is required but massive memory capacity is not.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F64als_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring a 64-core AMD EPYC 9V45 CPU at 4.5 GHz, 128 GB RAM, and top-tier benchmark performance for CPU-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F64als_v7 is a compute-optimized VM with 64 AMD EPYC cores, 128 GB RAM, and top-tier CPU and web serving benchmark performance.
tagline: A high-frequency, compute-dense virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors for CPU-bound workloads requiring minimal memory overhead.
bullet_points: Dedicated 64-core AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz
128.0 GB system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
Top-tier CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
No bundled local storage with premium storage capability
Suited for compute-intensive tasks, web serving, and CPU-based LLM inference
